prompt: The test passes even though the method is broken. What is the bug?
choices: abs(5) should return 0 for the test to be meaningful
The test only checks abs(5)==5 which passes even for a broken implementation; it never tests abs(-5)==5, so the bug is undetected
AssertionError should be caught with catch(Exception) to detect all failures
The test method must be annotated with @Test to run properly
explanation: Incomplete tests provide false confidence. Testing only positive inputs misses the core functionality of abs() — handling negatives. A proper test would also assert abs(-5)==5 and abs(0)==0. Good tests cover positive cases, negative cases, zero, and boundary values.
